oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Control of spinose ear ticks in dairy cows with permethrin spray
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Jardon, P.W.
Rico, A.
Gerry, A.C.
Karle, B.M.
Silva-del-Rio, N.
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: The spinose ear tick (Otobius megnini) is a one-host tick found in much of North America but mostly in the arid southwestern United States. Infestations in lactating California freestall dair­ies have been reported. While it is known that infestations can be irritating to cattle, there has not been a study quantifying production losses in lactating dairy cattle. The objective of the present study was to evaluate the effectiveness of a 0.50% per­methrin spray (Catron® IV, Elanco Animal Health) in reducing spinose ear tick infestation in lactating dairy cows and its im­pact on milk yield.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 213
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
